screens.title: Your First Line of Defense
screens.subtitle: Weak or reused passwords make it easy for attackers to access your accounts. Strong password habits can prevent most unauthorized access.
agenda: Learn how to protect your accounts
Learn how to create strong passwords

screens.context: You are a new hire logging into your work accounts for the first time. You need to create a password for your company email and internal systems.

interactiveImage.imageAlt: Password creation screen showing security requirements

explanations.title: Password123!
explanations.description: This is extremely common and easily guessed by attackers using password lists.

explanations.title: Aaron2067._.
explanations.description: Predictable patterns like name and birth year are commonly targeted in attacks.

explanations.title: CompanyName67.
explanations.description: Using company or personal information makes passwords easier to guess.

explanations.title: G7$kL!2pQz321
explanations.description: Randomized and long combinations are much harder to crack and significantly more secure.

screens.title: Clickable Password Breakdown

interactiveImage.imageAlt: Password options shown for users to inspect weak patterns

screens.instructions: Review each password and click on the parts that make it weak. As you tap, explanations will appear on the side.

clickableElements.label: Password
clickableElements.explanation: Common words like 'Password' are among the first combinations attackers test.
Simple number and symbol patterns are predictable and commonly used.

clickableElements.label: Aaron
clickableElements.explanation: Names are personal identifiers that make passwords easier to guess.
Years and birthday-like number patterns are often targeted in guessing attacks.

clickableElements.label: CompanyName
clickableElements.explanation: Company names or workplace-related words are easier to predict in a work setting.
Short, predictable number and punctuation patterns do not add much security.

clickableElements.label: Entire password
clickableElements.explanation: This password is long, randomized, and does not rely on personal or predictable information.

screens.title: What should you do next time?

cards.title: Use Strong and unique passwords
cards.description: Avoid reusing passwords across your personal accounts

cards.title: Avoid predictable creation patterns
cards.description: Avoid names, birthdays, company info, etc.

cards.title: Use a password manager
cards.description: Generate and store secure passwords to use (ensure that prompts 1 and 2 don’t occur)

screens.title: Module Complete
screens.reflection: Congrats on completing your interactive module!
takeaways: Common weak passwords and what to avoid
What makes a password strong and secure
Steps to take that will better protect your accounts

confidenceMeasure.question: How would you rate your confidence on this topic?
